name: Checkers

vat_indicator.description: Prices printed as R34.99A mean zero-rated (0% VAT). Prices with no suffix mean 15% VAT applies.

quantity_rules.description: Quantity is ALWAYS 1 unless the receipt explicitly shows 'n @' before the price.
quantity_rules.multi_unit_pattern: n @ Rxx.xx — e.g. '2 @ R1.40' means quantity 2, unit price R1.40
examples: CHECKOUT BAG CH  2 @ R1.40  R2.80  →  quantity=2, unit_price=1.40, total_price=2.80
TOM PST 115G (V)  4 @ R14.99  R59.96  →  quantity=4, unit_price=14.99, total_price=59.96

product_name_rules.description: Numbers and codes in product names are descriptors, NOT quantities.
patterns: 1S = single unit of that product type (e.g. AVO BAG 1S = one bag of avos)
1.5 = weight in kg (e.g. APL ROYL GALA 1.5 = 1.5kg bag of Royal Gala apples)
2KG = 2 kilogram bag — this is the pack size, not the quantity
(V) = variable weight item, priced by weight at till

weight_rules.description: For variable weight items, extract weight_kg if determinable from product name.
examples: APL ROYL GALA 1.5 (V)  R34.99A  →  weight_kg=1.5, per_kg_price=34.99/1.5=23.33
GINGER KG (V)  R25.48A  →  weight_kg=null (actual weight not on receipt), per_kg_price=null
BANANA BULK 1S (V)  R39.99A  →  weight_kg=null (1S is unit code not weight)

discount_rules.description: Discounts appear as negative price lines, often branded as XTRASAVE or similar.
examples: XTRASAVE HBD OATS  -R7.00  →  include as item, total_price=-7.00, category=discount
XTRASAVE FRESH MILK  -R5.00  →  include as item, total_price=-5.00, category=discount

store_name_rules.description: Always normalise store name to 'Checkers' regardless of what appears on receipt.

item_count_rules.description: The receipt prints TOTAL [n] where n is the exact number of line items. Count must match.
item_count_rules.example: TOTAL [21] means 21 line items including discounts and bag charges

prompt_notes: The RATE/TAX/GROSS/NET table at the bottom is ground truth for VAT. Use the TAX value from the 15% row as vat_total.
A = zero-rated on the price suffix is more reliable than guessing from product category.
Hot food counter items (e.g. SP VEG = Special Vegetable Soup) are 15% VAT — they are prepared foods.
Shoprite is a different store. Checkers and Shoprite are sister brands but separate stores.
